title: News and updates | Employment New Zealand
content:
## News and updates Ngā rongo kōrero me ngā whakahou
News and media releases from Employment New Zealand.
- 24 Jan 2025 South Waikato farmer ordered to pay $30,000 for employment breaches The Employment Relations Authority (ERA) has ordered a South Waikato farmer and his dairy farming business to pay penalties of $30,000 for breach of employment standards relating to 4 employees. Legal case
The Employment Relations Authority (ERA) has ordered a South Waikato farmer and his dairy farming business to pay penalties of $30,000 for breach of employment standards relating to 4 employees.
- 4 Dec 2024 Kiwifruit labour company and director penalised $100,000 for underpaying workers A labour supply company to the kiwifruit industry in the Bay of Plenty and its sole director have been penalised a total of $100,000 by the Employment Relations Authority after being found to have underpaid 3 employees over several years. Legal case
A labour supply company to the kiwifruit industry in the Bay of Plenty and its sole director have been penalised a total of $100,000 by the Employment Relations Authority after being found to have underpaid 3 employees over several years.
- 22 Nov 2024 MBIE teams visit dozens of Hanmer Springs businesses in compliance crackdown Teams from the Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment (MBIE) visited 26 Hanmer Springs hospitality and retail businesses following complaints of potential migrant exploitation. Migrant exploitation
Teams from the Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment (MBIE) visited 26 Hanmer Springs hospitality and retail businesses following complaints of potential migrant exploitation.
- Migrant exploitation
- 30 Oct 2024 Dairy owners must pay $16,000 in penalties for employment breaches A Piopio dairy and its owners must pay $16,000 in penalties after the Employment Relations Authority found them liable for 21 breaches of minimum employment standards which impacted migrant employees. Migrant exploitation
A Piopio dairy and its owners must pay $16,000 in penalties after the Employment Relations Authority found them liable for 21 breaches of minimum employment standards which impacted migrant employees.
- Migrant exploitation
- 15 Oct 2024 Workers exploited - New Plymouth business, directors must pay $86,500 A New Plymouth restaurant and its two directors have been ordered to pay $86,500 in wages arrears and penalties by the Employment Relations Authority after breaching minimum employment standards. Migrant exploitation
A New Plymouth restaurant and its two directors have been ordered to pay $86,500 in wages arrears and penalties by the Employment Relations Authority after breaching minimum employment standards.
- Migrant exploitation
- 13 Sept 2024 Napier developer loses bid to have $96,000 penalties ruling overturned A Napier developer’s bid to overturn an Employment Relations Authority (ERA) determination ordering he and his construction company to pay penalties of $96,000 has failed in the Employment Court. Migrant exploitation
A Napier developer’s bid to overturn an Employment Relations Authority (ERA) determination ordering he and his construction company to pay penalties of $96,000 has failed in the Employment Court.
- Migrant exploitation
- 2 Sept 2024 MBIE teams check Queenstown businesses for employment, immigration compliance Teams from MBIE have conducted a joint-agency operation visiting 11 hospitality and retail businesses in the Queenstown area following complaints of breaches of employment and immigration legislation. Migrant exploitation
Teams from MBIE have conducted a joint-agency operation visiting 11 hospitality and retail businesses in the Queenstown area following complaints of breaches of employment and immigration legislation.
- Migrant exploitation
- 15 Aug 2024 Restaurant director must pay employee more than $40,000 in wages arrears The sole director of a liquidated Taupo restaurant has been ordered to pay a former employee more than $40,000 in wages arrears by the Employment Relations Authority. Legal case
The sole director of a liquidated Taupo restaurant has been ordered to pay a former employee more than $40,000 in wages arrears by the Employment Relations Authority.
- 31 Jul 2024 Company and its banned director ordered to pay $48,000 in penalties for employment standards breaches A Hawke’s Bay company and its owner, who is banned from acting as a director or manager of any company, have been ordered to pay penalties of $48,000 by the Employment Relations Authority for breaching minimum employment standards. Legal case
A Hawke’s Bay company and its owner, who is banned from acting as a director or manager of any company, have been ordered to pay penalties of $48,000 by the Employment Relations Authority for breaching minimum employment standards.
- 26 Jun 2024 Know your public holiday rights and responsibilities this Matariki This Friday 28 June 2024 celebrates the rising of Matariki and MBIE’s Labour Inspectorate is reminding employers and employees of their rights and responsibilities during this public holiday. Public holidays
This Friday 28 June 2024 celebrates the rising of Matariki and MBIE’s Labour Inspectorate is reminding employers and employees of their rights and responsibilities during this public holiday.
